body.title: Consult sessions follow through multi-turn — today every session closes at turn 1
body.result: ConsultSession now basket-persistent — first stuck event opens; different stuck_reasons on same basket re-use the session (multi-turn). Conclude runs at _pe_close + _pe_escalate. New consult_sessions_recent MCP tool gives CC step-through observability. 5 new tests + 1 updated. Commit bfc4852b. Igor needs restart for MCP tool to register.
body.description:
Per D-consult-primitive design: 'Consult is conversation-shaped, not query/reply — Igor may follow up, reason over intermediate results, or conclude.' Observed 2026-04-24: every session in consults.log closes at turn 1. Design says multi-turn; implementation is effectively one-shot. Igor gets a next_question back from the peer LLM but has no mechanism to return to the same ConsultSession with new evidence.

What's missing: a trip condition that makes Igor continue the same session instead of closing. Candidates: if hypotheses are still too vague, if next_question is unanswered, if confidence threshold barely clears. Needs a design conversation before build.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/consult.py (session-close logic), wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (_maybe_consult_stuck may need to hold a session handle), TBD in design.
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not — design first, tests flow from that.
**Scope boundary:** IN — design + ship multi-turn trip condition. OUT — new consult problem-kinds, tier changes.
**Test plan:** new tests: multi-turn session with follow-up raw_text, assertions on hypothesis evolution across turns. Existing single-turn behaviour preserved as default.
body.rejection_note: Igor proposal rejected — targeted brainstem/core_patterns.py (wrong file per ticket description) with empty Plan. CC authoring correct implementation in consult.py + pe_chain.py.
